tests.name: EngineRouter picks the cheapest permitted provider and stamps result metadata
EngineRouter falls through a throwing provider to the next, then to deterministic
EngineRouter tries the cheaper paid provider, then on error reaches deterministic fallback
EngineRouter falls through a timing-out provider to the deterministic fallback
EngineRouter records a non-negative measured latency stamped over the provider value
EngineRouter returns PROVIDER_ERROR when the tenant allow-list permits nothing
EngineRouter returns PROVIDER_ERROR when every permitted provider fails (no deterministic allowed)
EngineRouter honours a per-call timeout override regardless of provider config
EngineRouter rejects a provider returning a malformed result and falls through (defence in depth)
EngineRouter routingFromConfigs drops disabled providers and maps fields
FalProvider maps person/garment/seed/num_samples and targets the try-on endpoint
FalProvider defaults num_samples to 1 and resolves garment from productId when none supplied
FalProvider forwards the context abort signal to the runner
FalProvider reads the first url from an images[] array output
FalProvider fails closed when the output carries no image url
FalProvider fails closed when the upstream url is not https
FalProvider lazily builds the runner via the injected factory exactly once
FalProvider default runner factory fails closed when FAL_KEY is absent
EngineRouter — latency arithmetic (mutation-hardening) stamps latencyMs as (now - started), an exact positive elapsed value
EngineRouter — per-provider timeout preferred over default (mutation-hardening) uses the candidate.timeoutMs (short) and times out before the long default would
EngineRouter — per-provider timeout preferred over default (mutation-hardening) does NOT time out at the short deadline when default is short and used (control)
DeterministicProvider produces a byte-identical result for the identical request (property)
DeterministicProvider produces distinct URLs for requests differing only in productId (property)
DeterministicProvider returns a contract-valid result with the deterministic provider id and zero cost
DeterministicProvider emits a renderable data:image/svg+xml base64 url that satisfies the result contract
DeterministicProvider embeds the deterministic SVG inline as the base64 payload (offline, self-contained)
DeterministicProvider fails closed when the context signal is already aborted
orderCandidates orders by cost ascending, then priority ascending, then id
orderCandidates breaks an exact cost+priority tie by provider id ascending
orderCandidates denies providers that are not allow-listed
orderCandidates drops allow-listed providers with no routing metadata or no implementation
orderCandidates dedupes a repeated allow-list entry
orderCandidates produces a total deterministic order regardless of allow-list ordering (property)
SelfHostedProvider POSTs the mapped body to /infer and forwards the abort signal
SelfHostedProvider normalises a trailing slash on the base url
SelfHostedProvider omits seed when the request supplies none
SelfHostedProvider fails closed on a non-2xx status
SelfHostedProvider fails closed when the response is missing image_url
SelfHostedProvider fails closed when the returned url is not https
runWithTimeout resolves with the attempt value and clears the pending timer
runWithTimeout rejects with a ProviderTimeoutError and aborts the attempt signal on deadline
runWithTimeout does not time out a hanging attempt before the deadline is crossed
runWithTimeout propagates a rejecting attempt error unchanged
runWithTimeout rejects immediately when the caller signal is already aborted
runWithTimeout aborts and rejects when the caller cancels mid-flight
runWithTimeout ignores a timer that fires after the attempt already resolved (single settle)
ReplicateProvider maps inputs and version, forwards the signal, returns the string output url
ReplicateProvider resolves garment from productId and omits seed when none given
ReplicateProvider reads the first element of an array output
ReplicateProvider fails closed when the prediction did not succeed
ReplicateProvider fails closed when the output has no image url
ReplicateProvider fails closed when the output url is not https
GoogleVtoProvider maps the instance and parameters and forwards the signal
GoogleVtoProvider uses an explicit garment image and defaults sampleCount, omitting seed
GoogleVtoProvider fails closed when there are no predictions
GoogleVtoProvider fails closed when the prediction lacks a result url
GoogleVtoProvider fails closed when the result url is not https
orderCandidates — priority tiebreak (cost tied) orders by priority ASC when cost is identical (kills the priority-branch mutant)
orderCandidates — priority tiebreak (cost tied) reverses correctly when the lower-priority provider is listed first
orderCandidates — id tiebreak (cost AND priority tied) orders by id ASC when cost and priority are both identical (kills the id-comparator mutant)
canonicalize is invariant to object key ordering
canonicalize distinguishes arrays from objects and preserves array order
canonicalize serialises null and primitives unambiguously
fnv1a32 matches the known FNV-1a 32-bit vector for the empty string and "a"
fnv1a32 is an unsigned 32-bit integer for arbitrary input (property)
stableRequestHash is a fixed 16-char lowercase hex digest
stableRequestHash is identical across repeated calls on equal requests (property)
stableRequestHash changes when a meaningful field changes
validateProviderResult returns the result unchanged when it satisfies the contract
validateProviderResult rejects a non-https result url (fail closed)
validateProviderResult rejects a negative cost
validateProviderResult rejects a missing required field
imageRefToInput passes an https url ref through unchanged
imageRefToInput encodes a base64 ref as a data URI carrying its MIME type
imageRefToInput fails closed on an unrecognised discriminant
